title: Watch dir TOCTOU race: symlink or file swap possible during 200ms sleep between event and read
description:
src/watch.rs watch_loop() deliberately sleeps 200ms after receiving a Create event to let the file finish writing:

    tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(200)).await;
    // ... then calls process_file which reads the file

An attacker with write access to the watch directory can:
1. Create a benign file (triggers the event)
2. During the 200ms window, replace it with a symlink to a sensitive file (or a different content file)
3. process_file reads the swapped content

This is a classic TOCTOU race. The probability is high because 200ms is a generous window for a local attacker.

Note: this compounds with broadside-0e74.14 (symlink finding). Even if symlinks are blocked, the file content can be swapped.

Mitigation: open the file with O_NOFOLLOW at event time and hold the fd open through the read, or use a staging directory pattern where files are atomically renamed into the watch dir.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
